_note: When the SAME event name is BOTH dispatched (typed `createEventDispatcher<T>`) AND bubbled (DOM or component) from the same component, upstream svelte2tsx emits TWO entries for that name in the events object literal: the bubble's projection (`mapElementEvent('click')` for DOM, `bubbleEventDef(…)` for component), AND a duplicate `'name': __sveltets_2_customEvent` LAST. Per `ComponentEvents.ts:283-286, 304` (addToEvents detects the same-name collision, sets the entry to CustomEvent<any>, AND adds the name to `dispatchedEvents` which `toDefString` emits last). TypeScript's duplicate-property-key resolution (last wins) makes the effective type for that name `CustomEvent<any>`. Native mirrors this in `lib.rs` by injecting the overlapping name into `untyped_names`, which the layered `Omit<prev, keyof curr> & curr` chain resolves last as `CustomEvent<any>`. Effect: a handler typed against the dispatcher's original payload (`CustomEvent<{ id: number }>`) bivariantly passes (any-bidirectional). A `(e: MouseEvent) => void` handler does NOT pass — `CustomEvent<any>` is not assignable to `MouseEvent` (different structural shape). Pre-fix native ran `Omit<dispatcher, keyof bubble> & bubble` only — bubble won exclusively (click → MouseEvent), with the inverse failure pattern (MouseEvent passed, CustomEvent<{id}> failed). Post-fix matches upstream's behaviour empirically observed on c4 control-rig FolderTree where the prior 'bubble wins' shape fired a TS2339 false positive on `detail.folderId` in `<Component on:menuOptionSelected={({detail}) => detail.folderId}>`.
